description: The ledger serves as the sole communication bus between agents. All agents write to the ledger when completing work that may affect other agents. All agents read the ledger to discover relevant changes. No direct agent-to-agent communication.

entries.description: Implemented full seed script with synthetic data: 2 tenants, 2 admins, 4 doctors, 8 patients, ~31 appointments, visit notes for completed appointments, billing records for completed appointments, 8 insurance verifications, and sample audit log entries. All data is synthetic. SSNs use placeholder encryption format (encrypted:iv:tag:cipher). Passwords use bcrypt-style placeholder hashes.

Implemented all API endpoints, middleware (auth JWT verify, audit logging, PII guard response interception), services (AES-256-GCM encryption, insurance verification with Redis cache, notification stubs), and utilities (Winston logger with PII redaction, Prisma singleton, Redis singleton). All routes are tenant-scoped, audit-logged, and PII-filtered. Zod validation on all inputs. Typed error handling. Graceful shutdown. New files: packages/api/src/lib/prisma.ts, packages/api/src/lib/redis.ts.

Implemented CI/CD pipeline (5-gate quality checks: lint, typecheck, test, security-audit, pii-scan), enhanced Dockerfile with Prisma generate and healthcheck, database backup and key rotation scripts, Terraform AWS infrastructure config, .env.example.

Implemented all web UI: API client with JWT auth flow (memory-only tokens, auto-refresh), AuthProvider context, 10 shadcn/ui components (button, input, card, badge, dialog, select, toast, table, label, tabs), 3 form components (login, appointment booking, patient intake), 5 data display components (stats card, appointment card, patient summary, visit note, billing row), all 8 pages (login, dashboard with role-specific views, appointments with CRUD, records with SOAP notes, admin overview, billing management, staff management). No PHI in client state. No localStorage/cookies for patient data.

Wrote 9 comprehensive test suites (2,260 lines): PII guard middleware (masking SSN/email, recursive field scrubbing, res.json interception), auth middleware (JWT verification, role guards), audit middleware (path parsing, async log writing), encryption service (AES-256-GCM round-trip, tamper detection, HMAC-SHA256 hashing), patient routes (CRUD, tenant isolation, SSN masking, role restrictions), appointment routes (CRUD, status transitions, availability, visit note requirements), auth routes (login/refresh/logout, anti-enumeration, account lockout), billing routes (tenant-scoped, claim validation, reporting), RBAC permissions (full matrix validation, scope checks, hierarchy). All tests use synthetic data only.

rules: Every agent MUST log completed work that affects shared types, APIs, schemas, infrastructure, or cross-boundary concerns.
The 'requires_attention_from' field must list the roles that should act on this change (e.g., ['frontend'] when backend updates shared types).
Entries are append-only. No editing or deleting previous entries.
Security-related changes must be tagged with 'security: true' for audit trail purposes.
Handoffs between agents happen exclusively through the ledger. The producing agent logs the change; the consuming agent reads it and acts.
